summary: First iteration full-stack testing for Pelangi Homestay. All 16 backend pytest tests PASSED (health, site/config, public blog, auth login/logout/me with httpOnly cookies, admin blog CRUD, contact submit + persistence, validation). All frontend Playwright flows PASSED via ingress URL: home load, Book Now hrefs (nav + hero -> https://pelangihomestay.com/book, target=_blank), WhatsApp floating button (wa.me/6285119459269, target=_blank), all 9 main routes, gallery filter tabs, blog listing (3 seeded), blog detail render, FAQ accordion, contact form submit + toast, admin protected route redirect for anon, login with admin@pelangihomestay.com / pelangi2026, create post -> appears in dashboard, edit loads existing data, delete, logout + subsequent redirect enforcement.

minor.issue: app.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware, ...) is added AFTER app.include_router(api_router). In FastAPI/Starlette this still works because middleware is processed via a middleware stack rebuilt on startup, but the conventional order is to add middleware before mounting routers. Not a functional bug; cookies were set and CORS worked in tests.
backend/.env has CORS_ORIGINS="*" but code only reads FRONTEND_URL (single origin). This is fine since credentials require an explicit origin, but the CORS_ORIGINS env var is unused/misleading.
Uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup')/('shutdown'). Consider migrating to lifespan handlers (non-blocking for now).
Returns 200 on create; standard REST convention is 201 Created (cosmetic).

issues: AuthContext calls GET /api/auth/me on every mount for anonymous visitors, producing a 401 in browser console on every public page load. Functionally correct but noisy — consider suppressing by only calling /auth/me when the user is on an admin route, or check a lightweight endpoint first.
A repeating 403 network error appears once per page (likely a Font Awesome kit / third-party asset). Not blocking; investigate origin to keep console clean.

action_items: Optional: only invoke /auth/me on /admin/* routes to remove noisy 401s from public pages' consoles.
Optional: remove unused CORS_ORIGINS env var or wire it to allow_origins.
Optional: migrate on_event startup/shutdown to FastAPI lifespan API.
Optional: return 201 on POST /api/admin/blog to align with REST conventions.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 500-line file mixing models, routes, auth, seeding — acceptable for MVP but should be split into modules (models/, routes/, auth.py, seed.py) if the CMS grows.
ObjectId is correctly excluded via post_to_out and manual pop in get_current_user — good.
Login endpoint has no brute-force lockout (auth playbook mentions 5-fail lockout). For a private admin-only CMS this is acceptable but noted.
seed_data_creation: Backend startup auto-seeds admin user + 3 blog posts. Playwright test created and cleaned up a 'TEST Playwright Post' via admin flow. Multiple TEST_ contact_messages remain in MongoDB but are non-destructive.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL with /api prefix. Cookies use secure=true samesite=none — must test over the public ingress URL, not localhost. Admin creds in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Playwright must accept confirm dialogs before delete: page.on('dialog', lambda d: asyncio.create_task(d.accept())). The 401 for /auth/me on anonymous public pages is expected (AuthProvider mounts globally). Public blog GET returns only published posts; unpublished posts are only visible via admin endpoints.
rca of the issue: No issues found requiring RCA. All requested features from the review request are functioning end-to-end.
